p1Defects: P1-1: No pset/retrieval world for Cluster 5 (Subtypes + Countability). Four worlds of new material (W20-W23) with no retrieval practice, violating the course's own lecture-example-pset pattern.

P1-2: W12 (BinaryRelations) boss is underspecified — offers two alternatives without committing, one is a counterexample (wrong for a lecture boss), the other tests only transitivity (ignoring reflexivity, symmetry, antisymmetry, PER).
p2Defects: P2-1: Left-inverse constructivity addressed only by docstring in W6 — should be a dedicated level.
P2-2: Proof-move map claims ℕ-induction practice in W23 but W23's spec doesn't mention it.
P2-3: Two back-to-back lecture worlds (W22, W23) with no example world between them. Overlaps with P1-1.
P2-4: Section 3 proof-move map table has '—' entries inconsistent with Section 8 transfer plans.
recommendations: Add a W24 pset/consolidation world for Cluster 5 that integrates subtypes, countability, and encodability. Include concrete examples (is ℤ countable? encode a specific pair). This fixes P1-1 and P2-3 together.
Replace W12's boss with an integrative theorem: 'a PER is reflexive on its domain {x | R x x}'. Remove the non-committal 'or' phrasing. This fixes P1-2.
Add explicit ℕ-induction practice to W23's proof-move goals. This fixes P2-2.
Upgrade left-inverse constructivity from docstring to a dedicated level in W6 or W7. This fixes P2-1.
Update Section 3 proof-move map table to match Section 8 transfer plans. This fixes P2-4.
notes: Review round 1. No P0 defects. Two P1 defects block passage: missing Cluster 5 retrieval world and underspecified W12 boss. Both are fixable with targeted plan amendments. The plan is otherwise excellent — full scope coverage, substantive proof-move map with 20 moves, sound world graph, complete example coverage, detailed transfer plans for 6 high-value moves. Fix the P1s and P2s, then re-review.
